  ◦ Removes rows where the 'Player' column equals 'Player' to eliminate repeated headers

• Drops rows with missing values in any of the key stat columns (PTS, AST, TRB, FG%)
• Returns the processed and cleaned DataFrame

def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_reg

def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    base_url_adv = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_advanced.html"
    )

    # Scrape per-game stats
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year
    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(

    df_adv = df_adv[

    ]
    df_adv = df_adv.dropna(
        subset=["BPM", "WS/48", "TS%", "VORP", "WS", "PER"]
    )

    # Merge per-game and advanced stats on Player and Year
    df_merged = pd.merge(df_reg, df_adv, on=["Player", "Year"], how="inner")

    return df_merged

    df_reg["Year"] = year
    df_reg = df_reg[

    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]

    # Scrape advanced stats

    df_adv = df_adv[df_adv["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_adv["Year"] = year
    df_adv = df_adv[
        ["Player", "Year", "BPM", "WS/48", "TS%", "VORP", "WS", "PER"]
    ]
    df_adv = df_adv.dropna(
        subset=["BPM", "WS/48", "TS%", "VORP", "WS", "PER"]
    )

    # Merge per-game and advanced stats on Player and Year
    df_merged = pd.merge(df_reg, df_adv, on=["Player", "Year"], how="inner")

    return df_merged

def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    base_url_adv = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_advanced.html"
    )

    # Scrape per-game stats
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year
    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid

    # Scrape advanced stats
    df_adv = pd.read_html(base_url_adv, header=0)[0]
    df_adv = df_adv[df_adv["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_adv["Year"] = year
    df_adv = df_adv[
        ["Player", "Year", "BPM", "WS/48", "TS%", "VORP", "WS", "PER"]
    ]
    df_adv = df_adv.dropna(
        subset=["BPM", "WS/48", "TS%", "VORP", "WS", "PER"]
    )

    # Merge per-game and advanced stats on Player and Year
    df_merged = pd.merge(df_reg, df_adv, on=["Player", "Year"], how="inner")

    return df_merged

def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model
    model = XGBRanker(
        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,
        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)

def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model
    model = XGBRanker(
        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,
        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)

data.original_code:
def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model
    model = XGBRanker(
        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,
        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)
data.updated_code:
def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Test multiple n_estimators values
    best_ndcg = -np.inf
    best_n_estimators = None
    best_model = None
    best_y_pred = None

    for n_estimators in [100, 1000, 2000]:
        model = XGBRanker(
            objective="rank:pairwise",
            learning_rate=0.1,
            n_estimators=n_estimators,
            max_depth=4,
            random_state=42,
        )

        print(f"Training XGBoostRanker with n_estimators={n_estimators}...")
        model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

        y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
        ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
        print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()} with n_estimators={n_estimators}: {ndcg:.4f}")

        if ndcg > best_ndcg:
            best_ndcg = ndcg
            best_n_estimators = n_estimators
            best_model = model
            best_y_pred = y_pred

    print(f"\nBest n_estimators: {best_n_estimators} with NDCG: {best_ndcg:.4f}")

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate using the best model
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = best_y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)

high_unstructured.summaryComponent: The main() function orchestrates the end-to-end process of training and evaluating an XGBoost ranking model for basketball MVP prediction.

astNodeRef.originalText:
def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Test multiple n_estimators values
    best_ndcg = -np.inf
    best_n_estimators = None
    best_model = None
    best_y_pred = None

    for n_estimators in [100, 1000, 2000]:
        model = XGBRanker(
            objective="rank:pairwise",
            learning_rate=0.1,
            n_estimators=n_estimators,
            max_depth=4,
            random_state=42,
        )

        print(f"Training XGBoostRanker with n_estimators={n_estimators}...")
        model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

        y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
        ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
        print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()} with n_estimators={n_estimators}: {ndcg:.4f}")

        if ndcg > best_ndcg:
            best_ndcg = ndcg
            best_n_estimators = n_estimators
            best_model = model
            best_y_pred = y_pred

    print(f"\nBest n_estimators: {best_n_estimators} with NDCG: {best_ndcg:.4f}")

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate using the best model
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = best_y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)

def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Test multiple n_estimators values
    best_ndcg = -np.inf
    best_n_estimators = None
    best_model = None
    best_y_pred = None

    for n_estimators in [100, 1000, 2000]:
        model = XGBRanker(
            objective="rank:pairwise",
            learning_rate=0.1,
            n_estimators=n_estimators,
            max_depth=4,
            random_state=42,
        )

        print(f"Training XGBoostRanker with n_estimators={n_estimators}...")
        model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

        y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
        ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
        print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()} with n_estimators={n_estimators}: {ndcg:.4f}")

        if ndcg > best_ndcg:
            best_ndcg = ndcg
            best_n_estimators = n_estimators
            best_model = model
            best_y_pred = y_pred

    print(f"\nBest n_estimators: {best_n_estimators} with NDCG: {best_ndcg:.4f}")

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate using the best model
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = best_y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)

def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot predicted vs true rank for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)
    # Order by player name alphabetically by true rank
    year_df["Name"] = year_df["Name"].astype(str)
    year_df = year_df.sort_values(["Rank", "Name"])

    plt.figure(figsize=(14, 7))
    # Prepare data for grouped bar chart
    bar_width = 0.4
    indices = np.arange(len(year_df))
    # Plot true rank
    plt.bar(indices - bar_width/2, year_df["Rank"], width=bar_width, label="True Rank", color=sns.color_palette("coolwarm", n_colors=2)[0])
    # Plot predicted rank
    plt.bar(indices + bar_width/2, year_df["PredictedRank"], width=bar_width, label="Predicted Rank", color=sns.color_palette("coolwarm", n_colors=2)[1])

    plt.xticks(indices, year_df["Name"], rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP True vs Predicted Ranks ({year})")
    plt.legend()
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")
